compiled_state.title: Contract freeze (in-window): exit-code contract, error envelope everywhere, event-schema versioning
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Rec 6 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md. The JSON Schema system (SSOT schemas, $ref-shared common.schema.json, coverage guard test_schema_coverage.py) is a strength — keep it. But three contracts agents depend on are unfrozen, and the breaking-change window after the 2026-06-09 internal announcement is the cheap time to freeze them: a 500-person rollout soon means mixed rebar versions sharing one origin/tickets.

## What
(a) Exit-code contract: 0/1/2/10 are load-bearing for agents but scattered through script headers — author one canonical doc (docs/exit-codes.md or a section in output-schemas.md) + a conformance test.
(b) Error envelope: extend the error_envelope schema to every command's failure mode so agents never parse stderr prose; error-shape changes are breaking — decide now.
(c) Event-schema versioning: docs/event-schema.md is the true wire format BETWEEN CLONES of different rebar versions. Declare it formally versioned; add + test a reducer rule that unknown event TYPEs are preserved-and-ignored (forward compat).
(d) Close the mcp_server.py pydantic-model-vs-JSON-schema equivalence pin fully (test_schema_outputs.py is partial).

## Scope
Contracts and docs + their conformance tests; no behavioral redesign. Anything that changes an emitted error shape lands inside the window.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Canonical exit-code contract documented + conformance-tested
- [ ] error_envelope covers every command failure mode; documented as the only machine-readable error channel
- [ ] Event schema carries an explicit version + tested unknown-TYPE forward-compat rule
- [ ] MCP outputSchemas pinned equivalent to the JSON Schemas in CI

comments.body:
EXECUTION DECOMPOSITION + RECORDED DECISIONS (dry-run feedback: scope too broad, contracts ambiguous):
Execute as FOUR sequential sub-efforts (file child tasks at start, one per letter, linked discovered_from this story; each independently committable):
(a) exit-code contract -> docs/exit-codes.md + tests/interfaces/test_exit_codes.py. SCOPE DECISION: all public dispatcher subcommands (the 41-arm inventory on ticket dotty-ditty-ripen); meta arms (help) excluded.
(b) error envelope. SCOPE DECISION: error_envelope applies ONLY when --output json is requested; text-mode stderr prose is unchanged. BACKWARD-COMPAT DECISION: shape changes land in-window with NO migration shim (zero external consumers); release notes flag it.
(c) event-schema versioning. STORAGE DECISION: a SCHEMA_VERSION constant in ticket_reducer + a version declaration in docs/event-schema.md; NO new VERSION event type and no version negotiation — forward compat is exactly "unknown event_type values are preserved-and-ignored by replay" + its RED test (unknown type does not error, ticket remains readable, unknown event retained for round-trip).
(d) MCP outputSchema pinning. SCOPE DECISION: extend test_schema_outputs.py until every MCP tool with an outputSchema is validated against its canonical JSON schema (enumerate via list_tools(); the gap today is coverage, not mechanism).
Order: (a) and (c) are doc+test only — first; (b) is the only code-wide audit — last; (d) anytime.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Story complete: all 4 sub-efforts landed RED->GREEN with tests + docs, committed to main.
(a) urge-index-zoom: docs/exit-codes.md + test_exit_codes.py; show/list unknown-opt -> exit 2.
(c) astir-plank-scuff: SCHEMA_VERSION + KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES; compaction preserves unknown-type event files; test_event_schema_forward_compat.py.
(d) wine-yield-scene: test_mcp_output_schema_coverage.py (list_tools()-sourced); list_epics/bridge_fsck now advertise outputSchema.
(b) large-comet-mica: error_envelope.exit_code + shared emitter; routed show/deps/transition/reopen/delete/create/get-verify-commands/claim/next-batch; test_error_envelope.py.
Breaking-window contract changes recorded in docs/release-notes.md.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
